name: TDD-STATE-MACHINE-1
title: rabbit-feature-touch Step 1 calls resolve-scope.sh directly instead of invoking rabbit-feature-scope skill
description: In the rabbit-feature-touch skill Step 1, scope resolution is performed by running resolve-scope.sh directly and dispatching a raw Agent with the resulting prompt. This bypasses the rabbit-feature-scope Skill tool entirely. The rabbit-feature-scope skill description states it 'emits a prompt for a default-model Agent; caller parses the JSON response' — meaning it is designed to be invoked via the Skill tool, not circumvented via its internal script. The consequence: any future changes to rabbit-feature-scope skill logic (pre-dispatch validation, output format changes, additional steps) will be silently bypassed by feature-touch executions. Fix: rabbit-feature-touch Step 1 should invoke Skill('rabbit-feature-scope', args) via the Skill tool rather than shelling into resolve-scope.sh directly.
